added_rules.content: When adding a new value to a column governed by a CHECK constraint, create a migration to DROP and re-ADD the constraint with the extended list before inserting data.
Never bundle changes from multiple beads into a single git commit — each bead changes must be in a separate commit with its own [bead-id] prefix.
Validate cumulative business invariants (running totals, balance limits) under a SELECT FOR UPDATE lock in the Guard phase to prevent race conditions from concurrent requests.
When rendering or serializing deterministic output, write golden fixture tests that assert byte-identical results from identical inputs to catch format regressions.
Run the idempotency_key duplicate check before opening a database transaction to avoid holding locks during the lookup.
When a schedule or job can fail silently, record every execution attempt (success or failure) in an executions table to make failures visible.
Use shared transactional helper functions (like record_change_in_tx) for cross-cutting concerns within the same database transaction — never duplicate audit SQL across features.
After adding a new domain type to a module, grep the codebase for CHECK constraints, enum lists, and event subject counts that reference the old set of values.
